name: Implement an LRU cache
task: Create /tmp/eval_lru/lru.py implementing an LRUCache class. Constructor takes capacity. get(key) returns the value or -1 if missing and marks it most-recently-used. put(key, value) inserts/updates and evicts the least-recently-used entry when capacity is exceeded. Both operations should be O(1).

validation.script: import importlib.util, time; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_lru/lru.py'); m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(m); c = m.LRUCache(2); c.put(1,1); c.put(2,2); assert c.get(1) == 1, 'get(1) after put'; c.put(3,3); assert c.get(2) == -1, f'2 should be evicted, got {c.get(2)}'; assert c.get(3) == 3; c.put(4,4); assert c.get(1) == -1, f'1 should be evicted now, got {c.get(1)}'; assert c.get(3) == 3; assert c.get(4) == 4; c2 = m.LRUCache(2); c2.put(1,1); c2.put(2,2); c2.put(1,10); assert c2.get(1) == 10 and c2.get(2) == 2, 'updating an existing key must not evict'; cap = 40000; c3 = m.LRUCache(cap); [c3.put(i, i) for i in range(cap)]; t0 = time.perf_counter(); [c3.get(i) for _ in range(3) for i in range(cap)]; dt = time.perf_counter() - t0; assert dt < 0.12, f'operations are not O(1): {cap} keys touched 3x took {dt:.3f}s (budget 0.12s)'; assert c3.get(0) == 0 and c3.get(cap-1) == cap-1, 'stress broke correctness'; print('OK')
